label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out how many siblings there are in total based on the given conditions. The problem says: "Every brother has twice as many sisters as brothers. Every sister has one fewer brother than sisters. How many siblings are there?" Alright, so I need to set up some equations here. Let me start by defining variables. Let's let B be the number of brothers and G be the number of sisters. So total siblings would be B + G. Now, the first condition: Every brother has twice as many sisters as brothers. Hmm. So, from a brother's perspective, how many brothers and sisters does he have? Well, if there are B brothers in total, then a brother would have B - 1 brothers (since he doesn't count himself). And the number of sisters he has would be G. The problem says that each brother has twice as many sisters as brothers. So: G = 2*(B - 1) Okay, that seems right. Let me write that down as equation 1: Equation 1: G = 2(B - 1) Now the second condition: Every sister has one fewer brother than sisters. So from a sister's perspective, how many sisters and brothers does she have? Well, the number of sisters would be G - 1 (excluding herself), and the number of brothers is B. The problem says that each sister has one fewer brother than sisters. So: B = (G - 1) - 1 ? Wait, wait. Wait, the sister has one fewer brother than sisters. Let me parse that again. If the sister has one fewer brother than sisters, then the number of brothers (B) is equal to the number of sisters (G - 1) minus 1? Wait, no. Let me think. If she has one fewer brother than sisters, then the number of brothers she has is equal to the number of sisters minus one. So: Brothers = Sisters (from her perspective)
